title: quercus chrysolepis subsp. tomentella
intro: Quercus chrysolepis subsp. tomentella, also known as the island scrub oak, is a subspecies of evergreen oak tree native to the Pacific coast of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The island scrub oak is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting immune function. Traditional herbal preparations of the island scrub oak include teas, infusions, and tinctures made from its leaves, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
